title: sphaeralcea fulva
intro: Sphaeralcea fulva, also known as the orange globe mallow, is a perennial plant species belonging to the mallow family, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Sphaeralcea fulva include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing relief from pain and discomfort. Herbal preparations of Sphaeralcea fulva include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
